pi_items.source: task.json description (line 5) and export_result.sh (line 7) both specify /home/ga/Documents/FreeCAD/finished_bracket.FCStd

Calculated from geometry: Z-profile path length 100mm × extrusion height 50mm × thickness 2mm = 10000 mm³ baseline. Subtracting ~73 mm³ per fillet × 4 corners ≈ 8927 mm³ final. Verifier.py (line 63-77) explicitly recalculates and uses hardcoded range 8500-11500, contradicting metadata target of 8000.
Field exists in metadata but verifier.py never uses this value. Verifier uses hardcoded tolerance range (8500-11500) instead of applying volume_tolerance to target_volume.

task.json description (line 5): 'Extrude the ProfileSketch by 50 mm'. setup_task.sh generates Z-profile sketch. export_result.sh (line 79) measures bbox.ZLength. verifier.py (line 88) checks 49.0 <= bbox_z <= 51.0.
task.json description (line 5): 'create a solid body with 2 mm thickness'. Volume baseline calculation: 100mm × 50mm × 2mm = 10,000 mm³ confirms thickness of 2mm.

task.json description (line 5): 'Fillet the four sharp corner edges of the bracket (the short edges at the ends of the Z-shape) with a radius of 5 mm'. export_result.sh checks for has_fillet flag. verifier.py (line 98) verifies fillet was applied.

privileged_info_summary: The task creates a synthetic 2mm-thick Z-bracket by extruding a Z-profile sketch (100mm path length) by 50mm and offsetting with 2mm thickness, then filleting four corner edges with 5mm radius. The expected output is saved as finished_bracket.FCStd. Verified dimensions: extrusion height 50mm, thickness 2mm, fillet radius 5mm. Calculated final volume is approximately 8927 mm³ (baseline 10000 mm³ minus fillet material removal), contradicting the metadata target_volume of 8000 mm³.
